      <title>Bucks Could Part Ways With Or Reassign Doc Rivers; Taylor Jenkins Emerging As Candidate</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284929/Bucks-Could-Part-Ways-With-Or-Reassign-Doc-Rivers;-Taylor-Jenkins-Emerging-As-Candidate</link>
      <description><p>The Milwaukee Bucks could re-assign or part ways with head coach Doc Rivers this offseason, according to Marc Stein of The Stein Line.</p>
<p>Rivers has a record of 95 wins and 100 losses in just over two seasons with the team. The Bucks hired Rivers in January 2024 after abruptly firing Adrian Griffin as head coach.</p>
<p>Rivers was also recently announced <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284901/Candace-Parker-Amare-Stoudemire-Doc-Rivers-Headline-2026-Hall-Of-Fame-Class">as an inductee</a> into the Basketball Hall of Fame for the 2026 class.</p>
<p>Former Grizzlies coach Taylor Jenkins has emerged as a possible candidate to be the team's next head coach should the Bucks replace Rivers.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 05 Apr 2026 21:27:20 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284929/Bucks-Could-Part-Ways-With-Or-Reassign-Doc-Rivers;-Taylor-Jenkins-Emerging-As-Candidate</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Rivers_Doc_mil_240226.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Pressure 'Mounting' On Arturas Karnisovas; UNC Remain Interested In Billy Donovan</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284930/Pressure-Mounting-On-Arturas-Karnisovas;-UNC-Remain-Interested-In-Billy-Donovan</link>
      <description><p>Pressure is "mounting" on Chicago Bulls executive vice president Arturas Karnisovas, sources told Marc Stein of The Stein Line.</p>
<p>With Karnisovas in his sixth year as a Bulls' executive, the franchise is scheduled to have "pivotal" meetings to discuss its direction and whether Karnisovas will be retained.</p>
<p>The team is set to miss the playoffs for the eighth time in the past nine seasons.</p>
<p>Stein adds that the Bulls are looking to retain Billy Donovan as head coach.</p>
<p>UNC is interested in hiring Donovan, though Donovan is not looking to make any decisions before the end of the regular season on April 12. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 05 Apr 2026 21:58:48 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284930/Pressure-Mounting-On-Arturas-Karnisovas;-UNC-Remain-Interested-In-Billy-Donovan</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Karnisovas_Arturas_chi_250619.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Luka Doncic To Receive Medical Treatment In Europe For Hamstring Strain</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284933/Luka-Doncic-To-Receive-Medical-Treatment-In-Europe-For-Hamstring-Strain</link>
      <description><p>Luka Doncic will head to Europe to seek specialized medical treatment in an attempt to expedite his return from a Grade 2 hamstring strain, sources told Shams Charania and Dave McMenamin of ESPN.</p>
<p>The decision was made in consultation with the Lakers and his own medical team.</p>
<p>Doncic suffered the injury <a href="jhttps://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284886/Luka-Doncic-Out-For-Regular-Season-With-Grade-2-Hamstring-Strain-Playoff-Status-Uncertain">last Thursday</a>, with the guard one game below the 65-game threshold required for end-of-season award eligibility. </p>
<p>Doncic is averaging a league-high 33.5 points in 64 games this season.</p>
<p>The Lakers also <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284902/Austin-Reaves-Expected-To-Miss-4-6-Weeks-With-Oblique-Injury">lost Austin Reaves</a> this weekend for 4-6 weeks with an oblique injury. </p>
<p> </p>
<p> </p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 05 Apr 2026 22:56:35 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284933/Luka-Doncic-To-Receive-Medical-Treatment-In-Europe-For-Hamstring-Strain</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Doncic_Luka_lal_260225.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>UCLA Beats South Carolina 79-51 For First Women's NCAA Title</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284927/UCLA-Beats-South-Carolina-79-51-For-First-Womens-NCAA-Title</link>
      <description><p>UCLA won its first NCAA women's basketball championship Sunday, routing South Carolina 79-51 in the national title game in Phoenix. The 28-point margin ranks as the third largest in Division I women's championship history.</p>
<p>The Bruins never trailed, building a double-digit lead after the first quarter and extending their winning streak to 31 games. Lauren Betts was named the Final Four's Most Outstanding Player.</p>
<p>"We got whooped. Not beat. We got whooped," South Carolina's Raven Johnson said.</p>
<p>Five UCLA players reached double figures. Gabriela Jaquez led all scorers with 21 points, 10 rebounds and five assists. Betts added 14 points and 11 rebounds.</p>
<p>South Carolina's three primary contributors, Joyce Edwards, Ta'Niya Latson and Johnson, combined for just 15 points on 5-of-22 shooting. The Gamecocks finished at 29% from the field, their worst shooting performance in tournament history.</p>
<p>UCLA opened the second half on a 12-3 run to put the game beyond reach. The victory ended a 48-year wait since the program's 1978 AIAW title, with that era's standout Ann Meyers Drysdale in attendance Sunday.</p>
<p>"My dream was to be at UCLA," Jaquez said. "We were determined, the core group, to do something UCLA hadn't done before in the NCAA era. We always believed. Job is finished."</p>
<p>Coach Cori Close, in her 15th season leading the program, became the longest-tenured single-school head coach to claim a first NCAA championship.</p>
<p>"It's immeasurably more than I could ask or imagine," Close said. "It's beyond my wildest dreams."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 05 Apr 2026 18:59:21 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284927/UCLA-Beats-South-Carolina-79-51-For-First-Womens-NCAA-Title</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Logo_UCLA.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Increased Belief Giannis Antetokounmpo Will Be Traded This Summer</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284928/Increased-Belief-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Will-Be-Traded-This-Summer</link>
      <description><p>Expectations are increasing that the Milwaukee Bucks will trade Giannis Antetokounmpo this summer, according to Marc Stein of The Stein Line.</p>
<p>The NBA is currently investigating the Bucks over their handling of the player participation policy regarding Antetokounmpo.</p>
<p>The Bucks told investigators that Antetokounmpo <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284900/Bucks-Tell-NBA-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Declined-Participating-In-3-On-3-Scrimmages-During-Return-Process">declined participating in 3-on-3 scrimmages</a> as part of his return protocol. Antetokounmpo has told that league that he is healthy enough to return from a hyperextended left knee and bone bruise suffered on March 15.</p>
<p>There was already a strong belief that the Bucks would trade Antetokounmpo prior to their latest disagreement.</p>
<p>Bucks governor Wes Edens also told ESPN that Antetokounmpo would either sign an extension or get traded this offseason, with Antetokounmpo describing the ultimatum as "<a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284911/Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Opens-Up-On-Bucks-Relationship-Calls-Ultimatum-Slap-In-Face">like a slap in my face.</a>"</p>
<p>Antetokounmpo described his strained relationship with the Bucks' front office, saying that he doesn't feel "comfortable talking to nobody."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 05 Apr 2026 21:20:47 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284928/Increased-Belief-Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Will-Be-Traded-This-Summer</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Antetokounmpo_Giannis_mil_251210.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Nate Oats Signs New Contract Through 2031-32, Will Be Top 5 Highest-Paid Coach </title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284926/Nate-Oats-Signs-New-Contract-Through-2031-32-Will-Be-Top-5-Highest-Paid-Coach-</link>
      <description><p>Nate Oats signed a new contract with Alabama that will make him one of the five highest-paid coaches in college basketball.</p>
<p>Oats' new deal will run through the 2031-32 season, according to ESPN.</p>
<p>Since Oats took over the program in 2019, he has guided the team to five Sweet 16 appearances and one Final Four run.</p>
<p>The school also earned their first No. 1 seed in the tournament in 2023. </p></description>

      <title>Al Horford, Quinten Post To Be Re-Evaluated Next Week</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284922/Al-Horford-Quinten-Post-To-Be-Re-Evaluated-Next-Week</link>
      <description><p>The Golden State Warriors will re-evaluate Al Horford next week, the team announced on Saturday.</p>
<p>Horford has been out with a strained right soleus.</p>
<p>Horford is averaging 8.3 points and 5.0 rebounds on the season.</p>

   <item>
      <title>Giannis Antetokounmpo Opens Up On Bucks Relationship, Calls Ultimatum 'Slap In Face'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284911/Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Opens-Up-On-Bucks-Relationship-Calls-Ultimatum-Slap-In-Face</link>
      <description><p>Giannis Antetokounmpo spoke candidly with the Milwaukee Journal Sentinel about his interpretation of his relationship with the Milwaukee Bucks franchise as they continue down a more acrimonious path. As Antetokounmpo becomes eligible for a pivotal five-year, $275 million contract extension in October, he and the Bucks are heading towards an inflection point. Bucks governor Wes Edens told ESPN that Antetokounmpo would either commit to signing the extension or would be traded this offseason. </p>
<p>"For me, it's like a slap in my face," said Antetokounmpo.</p>
<p>During the 2025-26 season, Antetokounmpo has had just one Zoom call with Edens and Jimmy Haslam, while also having an additional conversation around a game with Haslam. This lack of communication led Antetokounmpo to find out about the Bucks' position on his future through the media.</p>
<p>"It says a lot ..." Antetokounmpo said.</p>
<p>"Especially [as] somebody that has been this loyal, and I've showed appreciation, to the team, to the community, to the fans, to everybody that I've worked with, you know?" Antetokounmpo said.</p>
<p>"And that's almost, like, a slap in my face."</p>
<p>Antetokounmpo said that the Bucks have asked him to address trade rumors, which he did first at the start of the season and again in February. </p>
<p>"I'm here, I'm committed to the team, and I'm committed to Milwaukee  because I love this city," Antetokounmpo repeated in this interview.</p>
<p>His wish is this: "To the organization, the conversations that I have between me, myself, and my bosses &minus; it's private. It should not be in the media. If they're willing, they want to share it to the media, they can go ahead and do that."</p>
<p>Speculation on a potential trade intensified beginning at last year's lottery, but no actual trade negotiations became serious.</p>
<p>"I'm here, I committed, I'm wearing the jersey, and it is disrespectful to my teammates, to my coaching staff, to the people that I go and sweat with, and go to war with, to say, 'I don't want to be here' &minus; and I will never do that," Antetokounmpo said again this week.</p>
<p>"Now, what goes on in private rooms? It's not my job, it's not my responsibility, and it should not &minus; in no shape or form &minus; be shared in public. I believe in etiquette. I believe in human etiquette, I believe in basketball etiquette. So with my owners, I have addressed what I feel and what I believe the situation is.</p>
<p>"Same with my GM, same with my coaches.</p>
<p>"And for me, that [Wes Edens] comment was a slap [in] the face. But, it's my boss. So I just gotta keep on moving forward."</p>
<p>Antetokounmpo also suggested his contract is his best leverage to impact the situation. Previous major trades by the Bucks came in 2020 ahead of Antetokounmpo's first supermax extension when they acquired Jrue Holiday, and again in 2023 when they traded for Damian Lillard.</p>
<p>"Could they downplay my contract?" he said. "1000%. 'Yeah, he's a Buck for life. Yeah, whatever.'</p>
<p>"I totally agree with you. That's my one, of my leverage, let's say.</p>
<p>"But again, I have declared that [I'm a Buck] many times, and I've said the most-important thing, which I believe in, and I would say again."</p>
<p>Antetokounmpo's communication with the Bucks' front office has also become more strained. </p>
<p>"Nobody," he said. "I don't feel comfortable talking to nobody."</p>
<p>Antetokounmpo continues to talk to general manager Horst, but less than before.</p>
<p>"I tried this whole year to stay to myself, and also this past summer," Antetokounmpo said. "I was more closed off to myself. I don't know if that's because I don't feel comfortable opening up enough. Or if it's just, I just don't want to open up at this point.</p>
<p>"It's definitely not like before."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 05 Apr 2026 08:45:58 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284911/Giannis-Antetokounmpo-Opens-Up-On-Bucks-Relationship-Calls-Ultimatum-Slap-In-Face</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Antetokounmpo_Giannis_mil_250502.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Five-Star PG Brandon McCoy Jr. Commits To Michigan</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284921/Five-Star-PG-Brandon-McCoy-Jr-Commits-To-Michigan</link>
      <description><p>Five-star point guard Brandon McCoy Jr. announced his commitment to Michigan on Saturday night.</p>
<p>"Coach [Dusty] May's vision lined up with my vision and dreams, which are to become a pro and stay in the league long term," McCoy told ESPN. "He said I could fill up a stat sheet, play winning basketball, be an elite defender and play multiple positions."</p>
<p>McCoy is ranked No. 18 in the SC Next 100 for the 2026 class.</p>
<p>McCoy is the first five-star recruit since May took over at Michigan.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284910/Michigan-Defeats-Arizona-91-73-Will-Face-UConn-In-National-Title-Game">Michigan will face UConn</a> in the national title game on Monday.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 05 Apr 2026 13:44:17 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284921/Five-Star-PG-Brandon-McCoy-Jr-Commits-To-Michigan</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Logo_Michigan.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Charles Bassey To Sign With Warriors</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284917/Charles-Bassey-To-Sign-With-Warriors</link>
      <description><p>The Golden State Warriors are set to sign Charles Bassey, sources told Michael Scotto of HoopsHype.</p>
<p>Bassey averaged 2.8 points and 1.2 rebounds in five games with the Celtics this season. </p>

   <item>
      <title>Dusty May Commits To Michigan, Won't Leave For North Carolina Job</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284932/Dusty-May-Commits-To-Michigan-Wont-Leave-For-North-Carolina-Job</link>
      <description><p>Michigan head coach Dusty May has told university officials he is not seeking other coaching positions, according to ESPN sources. The move effectively ends any possibility of May landing at North Carolina, which had identified him as an early candidate.</p>
<p>May's pursuit was never serious given Michigan's run to Monday's national championship game against UConn. The Wolverines finished 36-3, claimed the Big Ten regular-season title and recorded 29 double-digit victories.</p>
<p>"I think it's well-documented how happy I am at Michigan," May said Friday. "Obviously, my private life, my personal life, my family, their happiness is very important."</p>
<p>May signed a contract extension last year that runs through 2030. Departing for another program this spring would trigger a $7 million buyout.</p>
<p>North Carolina dismissed Hubert Davis last month following five seasons. The Tar Heels surrendered a 19-point lead to VCU in the first round, marking back-to-back first-round exits.</p>
<p>Arizona's Tommy Lloyd, considered the primary target for the Chapel Hill post, agreed to a fresh five-year contract to remain in Tucson on Friday. With both Lloyd and May unavailable, Chicago Bulls head coach Billy Donovan is expected to emerge as a leading candidate for the Carolina vacancy.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 05 Apr 2026 22:39:51 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284932/Dusty-May-Commits-To-Michigan-Wont-Leave-For-North-Carolina-Job</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/May_Dusty_mich_260316.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Blazers Exploring Coaching Market</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284931/Blazers-Exploring-Coaching-Market</link>
      <description><p>The Portland Trail Blazers are exploring the coaching market to better gauge the team's options, sources told Marc Stein of The Stein Line.</p>
<p>Tiago Splitter is 40-37 as the team's interim coach, with Chauncy Billups placed on indefinite leave by the NBA due to a federal gambling probe.</p>
<p>The Blazers are currently eighth in the Western Conference standings.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284824/New-Blazers-Owner-Tom-Dundon-Sets-Higher-Standards-Addresses-Arena-Future-Front-Office">New owner Tom Dundon</a> previously addressed potential personnel changes, saying that the team "isn't where I think we can be."</p>
<p>"I don't like getting rid of people. I like to see who's capable of taking that next step," he said. "They need to worry about what they're gonna do." </p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 05 Apr 2026 22:05:16 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284931/Blazers-Exploring-Coaching-Market</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Splitter_Tiago_por_251025.jpg" />
   </item>

<p style="position: fixed; top: 0px; left: 0px; width: 100%; text-align: center; z-index: 9999; margin: 0px; padding-top: 10px; padding-right: 10px; padding-bottom: 10px; background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9);"> </p>
<p>The NBA regular season reaches a boiling point this Sunday, April 5, as the <strong>Golden State Warriors</strong> host the <strong>Houston Rockets</strong> at the Chase Center. Golden State (36-41) enters this matchup with their season on the line, currently occupying the 10th spot in the Western Conference and fighting to maintain their place in the Play-In Tournament. </p>
<p><a href="https://imp.i305175.net/c/3589123/3360402/11640?subId1=RealGM" target="_blank" rel="noopener"> <input style="background-color: #ea7910; color: #ffffff; border-color: #141414; border-radius: 65px; cursor: pointer; border-width: 3px; padding: 10px; margin: 10px;" type="button" value="WATCH THE WARRIORS VS ROCKETS" /> </a></p>
<p>Meanwhile, the Rockets (48-29) have been one of the biggest stories of the 2025-26 season, sitting in fifth place and eyeing a potential top-four seed. Houston enters tonight on a scorching five-game winning streak. However, the energy in San Francisco will be electric for a different reason: the legendary return of the franchise icon. Catch the action live on Peacock. </p>
<p>The headline for tonight is the anticipated return of <strong>Stephen Curry</strong>, who is expected to suit up after a 27-game absence due to a knee injury. His veteran leadership will be crucial as the Warriors look to fend off a Houston squad led by Curry's former teammate <strong>Kevin Durant</strong> and the elite interior presence of <strong>Alperen Sengun</strong>. Houston also features the explosive scoring of <strong>Jalen Green</strong> and rookie sensation <strong>Reed Sheppard</strong>.</p>
<p>Golden State's veteran roster, which now includes <strong>Kristaps Porzingis</strong> and <strong>Al Horford</strong>, will face a massive test against a Rockets offense that has been nearly unstoppable during their recent run. You can witness this high-stakes Western Conference clash streaming live on Peacock.</p>
<p><a href="https://imp.i305175.net/c/3589123/3360402/11640?subId1=RealGM" target="_blank" rel="noopener"> <input style="background-color: #ea7910; color: #ffffff; border-color: #141414; border-radius: 65px; cursor: pointer; border-width: 3px; padding: 10px; margin: 10px;" type="button" value="LIVE STREAM NBA ACTION ON PEACOCK" /> </a></p>
<p>Houston's ability to dominate the glass through Sengun and <strong>Jabari Smith Jr.</strong> will be vital, especially against a Warriors frontcourt that has struggled with consistency during Curry's absence. Golden State will look to rediscover their championship identity, relying on <strong>Jonathan Kuminga</strong> and <strong>Brandin Podziemski</strong> to provide the support needed for a successful Curry comeback. Expect a playoff-like atmosphere at Chase Center.</p>
<p>With postseason survival at stake for the Warriors, this Sunday night showdown is essential viewing for NBA fans, and Peacock is your home for the complete broadcast.</p>
<p><a href="https://imp.i305175.net/c/3589123/3360402/11640?subId1=RealGM" target="_blank" rel="noopener"> <input style="background-color: #ea7910; color: #ffffff; border-color: #141414; border-radius: 65px; cursor: pointer; border-width: 3px; padding: 10px; margin: 10px;" type="button" value="STREAM WARRIORS VS ROCKETS!" /></a></p>
<h2>How to watch Warriors vs. Rockets</h2>
<p>Sunday's matchup will be broadcast nationally on <strong>NBC</strong> and streamed live on <strong>Peacock</strong>. Local coverage is also available through regional affiliates. Tip-off is scheduled for 10:00 PM ET (7:00 PM PT). Fans can stream the game in high definition on Peacock, which offers access to national NBA coverage and live sports.</p>

      <title>UConn Advances To Third National Title Game In Four Years</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284909/UConn-Advances-To-Third-National-Title-Game-In-Four-Years</link>
      <description><p>UConn defeated Illinois 71-62 in the Final Four on Saturday night to make their third national title game in four years.</p>
<p>Tarris Reed Jr. lead the Huskies with 17 points. Braylon Mullins added 15 points, going 4-7 from three. </p>
<p>UConn lead by as much as 14 points in the second half before Illinois cut the lead to four points with less than five minutes remaining.</p>
<p>The Huskes took a ten point lead into halftime while also not committing a turnover in the first half.</p>
<p>Illinois finished the game at 106 points per 100 possessions, almost 30 points below their season average.</p>
<p>The last time a team won three national titles in four seasons was John Wooden's UCLA team in 1975.</p>

   <item>
      <title>Lauren Betts, UCLA Defense Beat Texas In Low-Scoring Final Four Semifinal</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284908/Lauren-Betts-UCLA-Defense-Beat-Texas-In-Low-Scoring-Final-Four-Semifinal</link>
      <description><p>UCLA ended Texas's tournament run Friday in a grinding 51-44 semifinal victory, securing the program's first-ever NCAA championship game berth. The Bruins will face South Carolina on Sunday.</p>
<p>Lauren Betts delivered 16 points and 11 rebounds to pace UCLA. The win avenged the Bruins' only defeat of the season, a 76-65 loss to Texas on Nov. 26, capping a 30-game winning streak since that setback.</p>
<p>"All you can ask for is an opportunity to play your best basketball for a national championship," UCLA coach Cori Close said.</p>
<p>Texas star Madison Booker finished with a season-low six points on 3-of-23 shooting. Her 17 consecutive missed attempts set a Final Four record for the longest individual scoring drought.</p>
<p>"We couldn't make a shot tonight, and that's been my fear the last three days," Texas coach Vic Schaefer said. "I think we feel like in our locker room, we let one get away. I think this one will haunt me as the coach for probably 'til the day I die."</p>
<p>The game ranked among the lowest-scoring in Final Four history, with both teams combining for fewer than 100 points, only the third time that has occurred. UCLA committed 23 turnovers, the most in a Final Four since 2008. Texas shot 30.8% from the field.</p>
<p>A 7-0 fourth-quarter surge, sparked by a Kiki Rice three-pointer and a Gabriela Jaquez layup, pushed UCLA's lead to double digits. Texas trimmed the gap to 47-44 with 55 seconds left before Betts rejected a Booker layup attempt with 20 seconds remaining, effectively closing the door.</p>
<p>"Last year we took that loss really hard," Betts said. "I'm just really proud of the growth and the way that we've held such a high standard consistently this year."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sat, 04 Apr 2026 22:27:30 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284908/Lauren-Betts-UCLA-Defense-Beat-Texas-In-Low-Scoring-Final-Four-Semifinal</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Logo_UCLA.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Steph Curry Says Managing Knee Injury Is 'New Normal'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284905/Steph-Curry-Says-Managing-Knee-Injury-Is-New-Normal</link>
      <description><p>Golden State Warriors guard Steph Curry discussed his frustration in dealing with an "unpredictable" knee injury ahead of his expected return on Sunday against the Houston Rockets. </p>
<p>Curry has missed 27 games with "runner's knee."</p>
<p>"I thought I was going to be out a week," Curry said. "Ten days max. [But] every time I got on the court or tried to push it in that first month, there was always a reaction. You just knew it wasn't healing as fast as you thought."</p>
<p>Curry, who completed a 5-on-5 scrimmage, was cleared by the medical staff to play on Sunday.</p>
<p>The 38-year-old stated that managing the knee would be a "new normal" as he continues his career.</p>
<p>"Yes and no," Curry said. "There's nothing structurally wrong with my knee. So it's not like I'm compromised out there. It is a new normal, though, if that makes sense."</p>
<p>Curry added that dealing with the knee was different from previous injuries because there was no set timetable. </p>
<p>"Hopefully win two play-in games," Curry said about his expectations for the rest of the season. "Then we can have another conversation."</p>
<p> </p></description>

      <description><p>The Boston Celtics are signing Ron Harper Jr. to a two-year contract, his agent told Shams Charania of ESPN.</p>
<p>Harper was previously on a two-way contract.</p>
<p>Harper is averaging 3.4 points and 1.6 rebounds on the season. </p></description>

      <description><p>The Atlanta Hawks are signing Tony Bradley for the rest of the season, sources told Shams Charania of ESPN.</p>
<p>The Hawks are waiving Caleb Houstan to make room for Bradley. </p>
<p>Bradley provides front court depth with <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/284862/Jock-Landale-Out-At-Least-Two-Weeks-With-High-Ankle-Sprain">Jock Landale set to miss at least two weeks</a> with a high ankle sprain.</p></description>
